title: cyathea nesiotica
intro: Cyathea nesiotica, also known as the Lord Howe Island tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to Lord Howe Island in Australia. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Cyathea nesiotica include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and treat various gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The herbal preparations of Cyathea nesiotica typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the leaves and roots, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
